summary: Large decomp-matching PR that replaced many stubs or near-stubs across THP decoding, fighter common/Kirby code, game-mode files, baselib synth, soundtest, stage, audio, and player bonus code. The bot report records +3428 matched-code bytes, +48 matched-data bytes, 10 new matches, 39 unmatched-item improvements, and 4 unmatched-item regressions.

bot_report: THPDec_80330158 93.95%, THPDec_803310CC 82.60%, THPDec_80331340 96.76%, THPDec_8032F8D4 58.08%; THPDec .sdata2 new match

evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: adds ftCo_800D730C, fn_800D9558, fn_800D8EC8, fn_800D9930 and include additions
bot_report: new matches fn_800D9CE8, fn_800DA8E4, fn_800DA054, ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Phys; improvements fn_800D9558, fn_800D8EC8, fn_800D9930, ftCo_800D730C

what_changed: Replaced or advanced many previously stubbed functions across 28 files, with 3659 added and 193 deleted lines.
Added THP decoder functions for JPEG/JFIF marker parsing, scan setup, and decode entry points; updated THP structs and prototypes.
Added recovered fighter-common logic for jump/catch and Link/Samus item callbacks using fighter vars, item vars, joints, collision checks, SFX, and effect spawning.
Added or adjusted type/header fields for fighter, THP, synth, soundtest, game-mode, player, and MTHP surfaces.
Bot report recorded 10 new matches, including grInishie1_801FC664, several Kirby and ftCommon functions, and THPDec .sdata2.

Bot report recorded 39 additional improvements in unmatched items, including large improvements in gm_18A5, gm_1601, gm_1BA8, synth, ftCommon, Kirby, THPDec, soundtest, ftdynamics, lbaudio_ax, ftcoll, and stage code.
Bot report recorded 4 regressions in unmatched items: lbAudioAx_80028690, gm_1BA8 .sdata2, un_80300B58, and lbAudioAx_8002838C.
smart_moves: Updated headers when new function bodies or struct fields were introduced, rather than keeping all declarations source-local.
Used typed temporary structs in THPDec for scan and decode info instead of only raw byte-offset pointer arithmetic.
Kept ordinary string literals inline in added code, including "JFIF" and the THP OSReport string, rather than converting them into generated data symbols in the shown excerpt.
Used loops for repeated parsing and component-table setup in THPDec_8032F8D4 and THPDec_803310CC.
Used fighter/item/JObj helper macros and accessors visible in the excerpt, including GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, ftParts_GetBoneIndex, and typed item special-attribute structs.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For broad unstub PRs, bot match output can be useful for triage: separate fully matched functions from partial-score improvements and explicitly track any regressions that landed with the progress.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report lists 10 new matches, 39 improvements, and 4 regressions

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering SDK-like decoder code, local temporary structs with offset-labelled fields can make large state layouts reviewable while preserving unknown semantics.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: THPScanComp, THPScanInfo, and THPDecodeInfo added with explicit offsets and pad fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: Header surfaces should move with recovered bodies when the diff proves new callable functions or fields are needed by the implementation.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: thp.h adds THPDec prototypes and THPFileInfo::x0C used by THPDec_80330158
changed_files.jsonl: multiple paired .c/.h updates for gm_1601, soundtest, plbonuslib, synth, lbmthp, and fighter type headers

naming_conventions.convention: Unknown functions retained address-style names such as THPDec_8032F8D4, THPDec_80330158, fn_800D9558, fn_800D8EC8, and fn_800D9930 when semantics were not established in the PR evidence.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: added address-style THPDec and ftCommon function names
bot_report: reports address-style function names for unmatched improvements

naming_conventions.convention: Locals in shown recovered code use short role names where the role is clear, such as fp, item, it, jobj, attrs, vel, grav, marker, len, ncomp, sH, and sV.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_Attack100 and THPDec added functions

naming_conventions.convention: Unknown struct fields are kept as offset-like names such as x0C, x70, x8CC, x8F0, and x223C when semantic names are not proven.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: THPFileInfo::x0C, THPScanInfo fields, THPDecodeInfo fields, fighter var uses

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A narrow MWERKS inline asm block writes GQR5 and GQR6 in THP decode entry points before bitstream prep and MCU row decompression.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: THPDec_80331340 and THPDec_803313D0 contain #ifdef __MWERKS__ asm mtspr GQR5/GQR6 blocks
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Global standards treat inline assembly as exceptional; the PR evidence shows its presence but does not include reviewer discussion justifying it.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in recovered ftCommon functions to steer stack layout.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_800D9558 uses PAD_STACK(0x14); fn_800D9930 uses PAD_STACK(0x8)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Bot report gives score improvements but no per-function objdiff explanation for each PAD_STACK use.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: UNUSED locals were retained in fn_800D8EC8, likely for matching local/stack behavior.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_800D8EC8 declares UNUSED u32 unused1 through unused4
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: No review comments or build logs explain whether these locals were necessary beyond the reported score.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Repeated component and marker operations were represented with natural loops in THPDec code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: for loops over ncomp, len-skips, component counts, and x8BC fill table in THPDec_8032F8D4 and THPDec_803310CC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence does not state whether alternative loop shapes were tried.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Track regressions explicitly in broad unstub PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2561 demonstrates that a broad unstub/matching PR can produce many new matches and improvements while still introducing unmatched-item regressions; curator may record this as a triage lesson if consistent with other PRs.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report 10 new matches, 39 improvements, 4 regressions

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use offset-labelled temporary structs for large unknown decoder state
accepted_candidate_records.text: The THPDec additions use THPScanComp, THPScanInfo, and THPDecodeInfo with explicit offsets and padding, showing a reviewable bridge for large unknown state layouts.

source_update_candidates.title: Inline asm in SDK-like THP decode functions needs narrow justification
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2561 contains MWERKS inline asm blocks to write GQR5/GQR6 in THPDec_80331340 and THPDec_803313D0. This fits existing caution around inline asm, but the slice lacks reviewer rationale. Curator may consider adding a THP/GQR example to the existing avoid-pragmas-register-asm standard only after corroborating with current source and objdiff evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential example for an accepted exception pattern, but this PR slice alone does not prove necessity.

source_update_candidates.title: THPDec.c owns THP decoder scan/decode-state bridge structs in PR 2561
source_update_candidates.text: The PR introduced THPScanComp, THPScanInfo, and THPDecodeInfo inside extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c while adding THPDec_80330158, THPDec_803310CC, THPDec_80331340, and THPDec_803313D0.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: THPDec.c added local struct definitions and related functions
source_update_candidates.reason: Could improve future lookup for THPDec state-layout recovery, pending curator validation against current source.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all changed functions fully matched; the bot report shows many remained partial improvements.
Do not infer that reported regressions were reviewed or accepted intentionally; no human review comments are present.
Do not promote PAD_STACK, UNUSED locals, or inline asm as generally acceptable tactics from this PR alone; the slice lacks per-tactic verification rationale.
Do not infer semantic meanings for x-prefixed struct fields or address-style functions beyond names and usage visible in the diff.
Do not treat merged state as proof that every tactic shown is a global style standard.
